title: chloraea bella
intro: Chloraea bella is a type of terrestrial orchid native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of glycosides and flavonoids in its roots and leaves. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Chloraea bella is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used topically in the form of a cream or ointment to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
